- [ ] Cada acceptance criterion da task tem teste correspondente
- [ ] Coverage do diff >= 80%
- [ ] Testes têm nomes legíveis (`it('persists user when email is unique')`, não `it('test1')`)
- [ ] Sem `skip`, `only`, `xit`, `xdescribe`
- [ ] E2E Playwright presente em fluxo crítico (login, checkout, pagamento)

- Cria arquivo de teste no path correto (segue `.specs/architecture/PATTERNS.md`).
- Nome do teste descreve **comportamento esperado**, não implementação:
  - Ruim: `it('calls userRepository.save')`
  - Bom: `it('persists user when email is unique')`
- Roda o teste. **Tem que falhar.** Se passa de primeira -> teste tá errado, refaz.

## Steps

1. **Identifique o cenário**. Liste o caminho feliz e os principais erros (input inválido, sessão expirada, 404/500, viewport mobile/desktop). Cada cenário vira um `test(...)`.
2. **Crie o arquivo** em `tests/e2e/<feature>.spec.ts`. Nome em `kebab-case` casando com a feature (ex.: `tests/e2e/login.spec.ts`).
3. **Importe fixtures padrão** do Playwright (`test`, `expect`) e qualquer fixture customizada do projeto (`tests/e2e/fixtures/`).

**Do NOT trigger** on:

- Pure read-only ask ("what does this function do?") — answer directly.
- Architectural decision / refactor amplo cross-file — that goes to `architect` agent + ADR.
- One-off shell command, lookup, install task.

          "final_output": "Okay, here's how you can achieve this, broken down by common scenarios and technologies.  I'll provide examples for:

*   **Database Queries (SQL)** - This is the most fundamental approach.
*   **Backend Code (Python - Flask/Django)** -  Illustrating how to apply the logic in a web application.
*   **Frontend (JavaScript)** -  How to conditionally render the link based on user data.

**1. Database Queries (SQL)**

This is the core of the solution.  You'll need to modify your SQL query to only include the audit log link for users with the 'auditor' role.

```sql
SELECT
    *,
    CASE
        WHEN role = 'auditor' THEN (SELECT '/audit-log' ) -- Or the actual URL
        ELSE NULL
    END AS audit_log_link
FROM
    users;
```

**Explanation:**

*   `SELECT *`:  Selects all columns from the `users` table.
*   `CASE WHEN role = 'auditor' THEN ... ELSE NULL END`: This is the conditional logic.
    *   If the `role` column is equal to 'auditor', it returns the audit log link (e.g., `/audit-log`).  **Replace `/audit-log` with the actual URL of the audit log page.**
    *   If the `role` is *not* 'auditor',

## Agents verify-loop convergence

Lower attempts = the model resolved the case earlier; 1 means it passed on the first try with no feedback. Max attempts capped per harness.

| Model | metric | avg attempts (cli+ag) |

| router_extract_params | P/P/P/P(1) | P/P/P/P(1) |

Format suffix `(N)` on cli+ag is the number of verify-loop attempts consumed (1–3). Lower is better; 1 means it passed on the first try, no feedback needed.

Raw counts above are real `vendor/bin/phpunit` exit codes against `sistema-sindico`. `results_exec_sindico.json` holds per-case pass/fail, tokens, latency and a phpunit tail for every side.

target, testable criteria, constraints, identical output shape)
Both sides emit the COMPLETE updated PHP file. The harness writes that file
into a working copy of sistema-sindico, drops a HIDDEN PHPUnit test (never
shown to the model) into tests/unit/Core/Hidden/, and runs `vendor/bin/phpunit`
on the WHOLE suite. Pass = every existing test plus the hidden one go green —

Usage:
  BENCH_BASE_URL=https://router.huggingface.co/v1 BENCH_API_KEY=... \
    BENCH_MODELS="Qwen/Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B-Instruct,Qwen/Qwen3-Coder-Next" python3 bench/run_exec.py
Models prefixed `local:` run on CPU via transformers.

> **Atualização (commit subsequente ao RFC inicial):** o planner default
> mudou de DeepSeek API direto pra DeepSeek servido pelo HuggingFace
> Inference Router. Razão: a maioria dos usuários já tem `HF_TOKEN`,
> reduzindo o atrito de "comprar mais um plano só pra planner". Quem
> prefere DeepSeek direto passa `SIMPLICIO_PLANNER=deepseek/<model>`.

prefix                  base URL                                env var      tipo
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
deepseek-hf/<model>     https://router.huggingface.co/v1        HF_TOKEN     OpenAI-compat
deepseek/<model>        https://api.deepseek.com/v1             DEEPSEEK_API_KEY  OpenAI-compat
openai/<model>          https://api.openai.com/v1               OPENAI_API_KEY    OpenAI-compat

Each case asks the model to ADD a new method to an existing src/Core class.
Existing tests must keep passing (no breaking changes). Scoring is by REAL
PHPUnit (vendor/bin/phpunit) on the full suite plus a HIDDEN test (never shown
to the model) that asserts the new method's behaviour across true AND false
states. Pass == entire suite green.

quebra, a saída do Playwright vira o feedback que guia a próxima tentativa. A
evidência fica em `playwright-report/` e nos prints que o spec captura (ver

**"I want to run it in CI / a script / outside Claude Code."** Path 2. Get an
API key from any of the providers above (OpenRouter is the cheapest way to
try multiple models behind one key), set `SIMPLICIO_API_KEY` +
`SIMPLICIO_MODEL` + optional `SIMPLICIO_BASE_URL`, run `simplicio task ...`.

class GoGinCrudExecutor(TaskExecutor):
    """Render a compact Gin CRUD router into internal/http/router.go."""

class PhpLaravelCrudRoutesExecutor(TaskExecutor):
    """Render compact JSON CRUD routes into routes/api.php."""

class LibCSTUnavailable(RuntimeError):
    """Raised when libcst is not installed in the active environment."""

class PythonAddFastApiRouteExecutor(TaskExecutor):
    """Add one small FastAPI route handler to an existing API module."""

class PythonAddOrmFieldExecutor(TaskExecutor):
    """Add one SQLAlchemy 2.0 ``Mapped[...]`` field to an existing model."""

class PythonAddPydanticSchemaExecutor(TaskExecutor):
    """Generate ``XCreate``, ``XUpdate``, and ``XRead`` Pydantic schemas."""

class PythonAddPytestTestExecutor(TaskExecutor):
    """Generate one minimal pytest happy-path test for a Python function."""

def registered_executors() -> list[TaskExecutor]:
    return list(_DEFAULT_EXECUTORS)

class RustAxumCrudExecutor(TaskExecutor):
    """Render a compact Axum CRUD API into the scaffold's main.rs."""

class TypeScriptAddNextPageExecutor(TaskExecutor):
    """Create a small typed CRUD page for Next.js app-router projects."""

class TypeScriptAddNextRouteExecutor(TaskExecutor):
    """Create small JSON route handlers for Next.js app-router API routes."""

class PlannerError(RuntimeError):
    """The planner could not produce a schema-valid plan within retry budget."""

class RecipeError(ValueError):
    """Base class for recipe loading, matching, and rendering failures."""

public partial class Program;

## Project structure
- Keep supervision under the application module.
- Keep web routing in `lib/app_web/router.ex`.
- Put contexts in `lib/app/` once domain boundaries appear.

<project_name>/
|-- mix.exs
|-- lib/app_web/router.ex
|-- lib/app_web/controllers/health_controller.ex
`-- README.md

## Testing
- Use `httptest` against the Echo router.
- Keep tests package-local to handlers while the API is small.
- Run `go test ./...` and `go vet ./...`.

<project_name>/
|-- cmd/server/main.go
|-- internal/http/router.go
|-- internal/http/router_test.go
|-- go.mod

- `cmd/server/main.go` creates the router and starts the HTTP server.
- `internal/http/router.go` owns route registration.
- One file per resource handler under `internal/http/`.
- Keep domain logic out of handlers when it grows beyond a tiny first draft.

- Put HTTP controllers under `app/Http/Controllers`.
- Register API routes in `routes/api.php`.
- Keep request validation close to controllers until a form request class is
  actually reused.
- Use feature tests under `tests/Feature` for API behavior.

## Project structure
- Use PSR-4 autoloading from `src/`.
- Keep tests under `tests/` with matching class names.
- Keep framework adapters outside domain classes.

def test_hello() -> None:
    result = CliRunner().invoke(app, ["hello", "--name", "Simplicio"])

## API design
- Use Django REST Framework serializers for request and response validation.
- Use class-based viewsets for CRUD resources and explicit function views for
  narrow health or webhook endpoints.
- Keep migrations small and committed with the model change.

## Typing & validation
- All public function signatures fully typed; mypy-friendly even though we don't ship mypy by default
- Pydantic v2 models for every request/response body
- Path/query params use `Annotated[..., Query(...)]` form, not raw default args

def create_app() -> FastAPI:

    app = Flask(__name__)

def test_health() -> None:
    client = create_app().test_client()
